data.title: Wayfinder: The remaining PROMPT rewards for PRIME stakers will be distributed every Thursday until June 2027
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Wayfinder Foundation officially published on X that with the completion of the Genesis TGE, the Foundation now hopes to formally determine the future distribution schedule of the PRIME staking (cache) pool. The distribution will be carried out every Thursday, and the specific time point has not yet been determined, and it may be adjusted due to various factors, but the Foundation promises to distribute once a week before June 2027. 
Previously, Wayfinder planned to distribute 40% of PROMPT tokens to PRIME staking users. Only about 28% of them were distributed during the Genesis airdrop, and the remaining about 72% of PROMPT tokens are still in the pool to be mined.

data.title: Ethereum dApp fee revenue exceeds $1 billion in Q1 2025, ranking first
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Token Terminal data shows that Ethereum continues to maintain its leading position in decentralized application (dApp) platforms. In the first quarter of 2025, the network's dApp fee income reached US$1.014 billion. Ranked second is Coinbase's Layer2 network Base, whose dApp fee income is US$193 million, reflecting a certain growth, but there is still a gap compared with Ethereum. BNB Chain's dApp fee income is US$170 million, and Arbitrum ecosystem fee income is US$73.8 million. Avalanche's C-Chain ranks in the top five, with its dApp fee income of US$27.68 million. (Bitcoin.com)

data.title: EigenLayer announced that the penalty mechanism will be launched on the main network on April 17
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, Ethereum re-staking protocol EigenLayer announced that the slashing mechanism will be launched on the mainnet on April 17, and the active verification service (AVS) will be able to build verifiable, trustless applications; at the same time, operators and stakers need to take responsibility.
Slashing is a way for the PoS protocol to economically punish a node or a group of nodes for executing a strategy that can be proven to be different from a given protocol specification.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Highlights Privacy Concerns in the Age of AI
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Vitalik Buterin has expressed concerns about privacy in a new blog post, stating that privacy can no longer be ignored. He noted that artificial intelligence has significantly enhanced the ability to collect and analyze centralized data, as well as expanded the range of data we voluntarily share.

Buterin emphasized that technologies such as zero-knowledge proofs (ZK), fully homomorphic encryption (FHE), and obfuscation techniques have surpassed the imaginations of the 1990s cypherpunks. He warned that the most pressing risk is that privacy could reach a historic low, creating an imbalance where the most powerful individuals and nations can access everyone's data, while others remain largely uninformed.

data.title: Corporate Bitcoin Adoption Rises in First Quarter of 2025
data.description:
According to PANews, Cointelegraph reports that Bitwise has released data on corporate Bitcoin adoption for the first quarter of 2025. During this period, publicly traded companies acquired a total of 95,431 Bitcoins, marking a 16.11% increase compared to the previous quarter. This acquisition brings the total Bitcoin holdings of these companies to 688,000, representing 3.28% of the total supply of 21 million Bitcoins.

The number of publicly listed companies holding Bitcoin has risen to 79, showing a 17.91% increase from the previous quarter. Additionally, 12 new companies have started purchasing Bitcoin in this quarter, further contributing to the growing trend of corporate adoption of the cryptocurrency.

data.title: Fed's Bostic: Economy is in a state of substantial stagnation
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Fed's Bostic: Businesses and households lack confidence in making large investments, the uncertainty ahead is becoming more and more serious, we still have a long way to go in terms of inflation, inflation is still far above the target level, and the economy is in a state of substantial stagnation. (Jinshi)

data.title: OpenSea announces that it will launch NFT trading on Solana chain
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, OpenSea announced that Solana token trading is now online for some closed beta users on OS2, and will be gradually launched to more users in the next few weeks. Currently, tokens can be traded, and NFT trading will be launched later.

data.title: KiloEx: The platform has been suspended and the vulnerability has been contained
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News KiloEx issued a statement on the hacking incident, saying, "KiloEx Vault has been hacked. Urge all partner protocols and platforms to immediately blacklist the address to prevent further malicious activity. The vulnerability has been contained. The team has immediately suspended the use of the platform and is working with security partners to track the flow of funds. A bounty program will be released. A full report is being prepared." 
Previously reported, KiloEx was suspected of being hacked, involving about 7 million US dollars.

data.title: South Korea blocks 14 cryptocurrency exchanges from Apple App Store
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: The Financial Services Commission (FSC) of South Korea announced on April 11 that the apps of 14 crypto exchanges including KuCoin and MEXC were blocked in the Apple App Store due to unregistered operations. Previously, the Google Store had blocked 17 unregistered platforms on March 26.
The FSC said that these platforms are suspected of serving Korean users as overseas unregistered virtual asset operators, posing risks of money laundering and user losses. The Financial Information Analysis Institute (FIU) will continue to promote the banning of websites and applications of such platforms. According to FSC regulations, unreported virtual asset service activities are criminal offenses, punishable by up to five years in prison and a fine of 50 million won (about $35,000). (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Japanese medical services company SBC Medical announced the purchase of 5 BTC
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Japanese medical service company SBC Medical Group Holdings announced that based on the announcement of the purchase of Bitcoin disclosed on February 12, 2025, as of April 14, 2025, it has completed the purchase of 5 BTC (approximately 60 million yen/400,000 US dollars). 
Earlier in February, SBC Medical announced plans to purchase 1 billion yen (about 6.7 million US dollars) worth of Bitcoin to achieve the long-term strategic goal of asset diversification and fighting inflation, which is expected to be completed between February and May 2025.
